title: Implement agent step execution loop with ACP session support
description:
Create internal/wrapup/agent_steps.go with the step-checked execution loop.

Core type:
  type StepResult struct {
      Title     string
      Status    string  // completed, failed, escalated
      Cost      float64
      TokensIn  int
      TokensOut int
      Duration  time.Duration
      Attempts  int
      Error     error
  }

Progress persistence:
  type StepProgress struct {
      EpicID    string
      StartedAt time.Time
      Steps     []StepProgressEntry  // status, attempts, metrics per step
  }
  - saveProgress(logsDir, epicID, results) — writes to .tick/logs/wrapup-progress/<epic-id>.json after each step
  - loadProgress(logsDir, epicID) — loads for crash recovery, skips completed steps

WrapupRunner struct with:
  WorkDir, TickDir, Timeout, MaxRetriesPerStep (default 2)

Methods:
  RunAgentSteps(ctx, steps []WrapupStep, agentImpl agent.Agent) ([]StepResult, error)
    - If agent implements SessionAgent: open one ACP session, send each step as a Prompt() call. Agent retains context across steps.
    - If not SessionAgent: fall back to one-shot Run() per step (loses cross-step context but still enforces completion).
    - For each step: build prompt via BuildStepPrompt, send to agent, parse output for STEP_DONE signal. If missing, re-prompt via BuildRetryPrompt up to MaxRetriesPerStep times. On ESCALATE signal, mark escalated and continue. Save progress after each step.

Files: internal/wrapup/agent_steps.go, internal/wrapup/agent_steps_test.go
Tests: mock SessionAgent, verify retry on missing STEP_DONE, verify ESCALATE handling, verify one-shot fallback, verify crash recovery from progress file.
closed_reason: Implemented agent step execution loop in internal/wrapup/agent_steps.go with AgentStepResult, StepProgress types, WrapupRunner.RunAgentSteps method. Supports SessionAgent (single ACP session across steps) and one-shot fallback. Retry logic on missing STEP_DONE signal, ESCALATE handling, crash recovery via progress persistence in .tick/logs/wrapup-progress/. 8 tests covering all paths.
